name: pytorch-grad-cam (Gradient-based CNN Visualization)

performance.notes: Single forward+backward pass; minimal overhead; GPU supported

agent_readiness.notes: Gradient-based class activation map visualization for CNNs and ViTs. from pytorch_grad_cam import GradCAM, HiResCAM, EigenCAM. from pytorch_grad_cam.utils.image import show_cam_on_image. cam = GradCAM(model=model, target_layers=[model.layer4[-1]]). grayscale_cam = cam(input_tensor=input, targets=[ClassifierOutputTarget(class_idx)]). visualization = show_cam_on_image(rgb_img, grayscale_cam[0]). Methods: GradCAM, GradCAM++, XGradCAM, AblationCAM, ScoreCAM, HiResCAM, EigenCAM. Vision Transformer support. Batch processing. Mask generation for segmentation. Most popular CNN visualization library in Python.

gotchas: Requires access to intermediate layer (target_layers) — must know model architecture
Heatmaps are approximate — different methods give different results
Not suitable for NLP models (use Captum or SHAP instead)
best_when: Visualizing what CNN regions activate for a prediction
Medical imaging model debugging and validation
Demonstrating model behavior for non-technical stakeholders
Fine-tuning debugging when CNN focuses on wrong regions
avoid_when: NLP models (Captum or SHAP TextExplainer better)
When precise attribution needed (Captum Integrated Gradients more rigorous)
summary: pytorch-grad-cam generates gradient-based heatmaps for CNN/ViT predictions. GradCAM(model, target_layers=[model.layer4[-1]])(input, targets=[ClassifierOutputTarget(idx)]). Returns grayscale heatmap overlaid on image via show_cam_on_image(). Methods: GradCAM, GradCAM++, EigenCAM, ScoreCAM. Vision Transformer support. Most popular CNN visualization library. Essential for medical imaging and debugging what model 'sees'. For NLP explanations use Captum or SHAP.
